title: Morning Korean Skincare Routine for Oily Skin
excerpt: A 5-step Korean morning routine for oily skin — balances sebum, controls shine, and protects your barrier in under 10 minutes.
intro: Oily skin in the morning needs a routine that respects your skin barrier while preventing the all-day shine cycle. This 5-step Korean approach focuses on lightweight, oil-balancing actives — no heavy creams, no harsh stripping. Each step layers quickly so your sunscreen and makeup sit perfectly.

steps.title: Water Cleanser
steps.whyThisStep: Morning skin needs gentle cleansing to remove overnight sebum and product residue without stripping. This low-pH formula respects your skin barrier — the foundation for oil control all day.
steps.tip: Use lukewarm water — hot water strips your barrier and triggers more oil production.

steps.title: Toner
steps.whyThisStep: Heartleaf calms inflammation that often comes with oily skin, while gently balancing pH. Skip alcohol-heavy 'pore tightening' toners — they backfire.
steps.tip: Pat into damp skin with hands, not cotton pads, to avoid friction.

steps.title: Niacinamide Serum
steps.whyThisStep: Niacinamide is the most-studied active for regulating sebum production. After 4 weeks of consistent use, oil reduction is measurable.
steps.tip: 2-3 drops max — more isn't more. Apply to damp skin for better absorption.

steps.title: Lightweight Moisturizer
steps.whyThisStep: Oily skin still needs moisture — skipping it triggers MORE oil production. This water-based cream hydrates without weighing skin down.
steps.tip: If your skin feels even slightly tight, you need this step. Don't skip it.

steps.title: Sunscreen
steps.whyThisStep: Non-negotiable — even on cloudy days, UV triggers sebum oxidation that makes oily skin look duller. This hybrid SPF50+ is lightweight and doesn't pill under makeup.
steps.tip: Use a full quarter-teaspoon for your face. Most people under-apply by 75%.

outro: This 5-step routine takes under 10 minutes once you get the rhythm. Stick with it for 4-6 weeks before evaluating — niacinamide and heartleaf both need time to show full effect on sebum regulation.
